name: Energy & Utilities official sources
notes: Energy carries the site's most price-perishable public numbers: electricity tariffs, petrol/diesel/kerosene pump prices, LPG cylinder prices, water tariffs and solar kit costs. Fuel and FX move faster than the review cadence.

data/energy/country-energy-index.js carries a single lastUpdated stamp for all 54 markets. That is a floor, not a per-figure date. Treat any price older than highRiskCadenceDays as planning-grade, and never present a bundled default as a current rate.
Never update a tariff, pump price, LPG price or water rate because a URL's content hash changed. Open the page and read the gazetted notice, exactly as .claude/rules/government.md requires.
A regulator source always outranks a vendor, installer or aggregator page for the same claim. Solar kit prices and PAYGO terms are commercial and are recorded as unsourced by design.

Only 12 of 54 markets have a bound electricity-regulator URL today. The other 42 are recorded in gaps.regulatorsWithoutUrl as known liabilities, not to-dos. Do not delete one without either finding the source or showing the figure as planning-grade.

The bug class to watch, per .claude/rules/telecom.md: `country.fuel[type] || 0` turns missing data into free, which then wins a cost comparison. The energy tools already force a user price or show "Check tariff" instead of zero — keep that pattern.

sources.title: NERC — Nigeria energy regulator
sources.notes: Electricity regulator for Nigeria. Authoritative for the residential/commercial/industrial tariff and any published bands. Read the current gazetted tariff order or notice before changing a rate — a changed page is not a rate change.

sources.title: EPRA — Kenya energy regulator
sources.notes: Electricity regulator for Kenya. Authoritative for the residential/commercial/industrial tariff and any published bands. Read the current gazetted tariff order or notice before changing a rate — a changed page is not a rate change. This regulator also publishes the monthly maximum pump prices used by the fuel tools.

sources.title: National Petroleum Authority — Ghana downstream fuel prices
sources.notes: Ghana regulates downstream petroleum through the NPA pricing window, not through PURC. Bind Ghana fuel figures here, electricity figures to PURC.

unsourcedClaims.why: Pump prices are the most perishable numbers in the dataset. Most downstream markets are deregulated or set prices through a petroleum regulator not yet bound here. The fuel tools already force a user-entered pump price and label the bundled value as a dated default; keep it that way.
Retail LPG is largely deregulated and cylinder-size dependent. No regulator binding exists; treat as an editable planning default, never as a fixed price.
Water tariffs are set by municipal/utility bodies and vary sub-nationally within a country. Only Ghana (PURC) is bound. The single national figure is a planning proxy.
Solar kit prices are installer quotes, not a regulated tariff. They move with FX, panel and lithium prices. The ROI tools treat these as replaceable quote inputs.
PAYGO terms are commercial provider offers, not a published rate. Bound to no authority; informational only.
